items.title: Fit imputers on training data only
items.avoid: Computing global medians, modes, or KNN neighbors on concatenated train+validation+test frames.
items.prefer: Wrap imputers in a Pipeline fit on X_train; persist and apply the same statistics at inference.
items.why: Preprocessing must not observe holdout distributions; otherwise offline metrics are optimistically biased.
items.example:
```python
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
pipe = Pipeline([("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")), ("model", clf)])
pipe.fit(X_train, y_train)
```

items.title: Audit before choosing a strategy
items.avoid: Defaulting every column to median impute without checking missing % or patterns.
items.prefer: Report per-column missing rates, plot missingness vs observed fields, then pick delete, impute, indicator, or native NaN handling.
items.why: Mechanism and sparsity determine whether deletion, group impute, or tree-native gaps is safest.
items.example: A column with 80% missing optional `referral_code` is a candidate for drop or a dedicated "none" category—not median fill.

items.title: Impute before scale in numeric pipelines
items.avoid: Fitting StandardScaler on raw columns that still contain NaN.
items.prefer: Order steps impute → encode → scale so every scaler input is finite and train-fitted.
items.why: Scalers need defined mean and variance; imputation makes that computation well-defined and reproducible.
items.example: ColumnTransformer: `("num", Pipeline([imputer, scaler]), numeric_cols)` fit on train only.

items.title: Preserve informative absence when plausible
items.avoid: Flattening structurally missing fields to zero without an indicator or separate category.
items.prefer: Add a missingness flag or keep NaN for tree models when absence correlates with the label.
items.why: MNAR and optional-field patterns often mean "missing" is its own signal, not noise.
items.example: For `employer_tenure`, add `employer_tenure_was_missing` alongside median impute for self-employed applicants.
